summary.brief: Delos-Santos v. Kaisman. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: The amended complaint and the bill of particulars alleged, among other things, that the defendant performed an endoscopic lumbar discectomy on the right side of the plaintiff's body, despite the plaintiff seeking treatment to address pain and discomfort on the left side of her body
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The amended complaint and the bill of particulars further alleged that the defendant failed to inform her of the possible risks associated with preforming an endoscopic lumbar discectomy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The defendant failed to establish his prima facie entitlement to judgment as a matter of law dismissing the cause of action to recover damages for medical malpractice insofar as asserted against him.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Supreme Court properly denied his motion for summary judgment dismissing the amended complaint insofar as asserted against him, regardless of the sufficiency of the plaintiff's opposition papers.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The affidavit of the defendant's expert failed to address and rebut all of the specific allegations of medical malpractice set forth in the amended complaint and verified bill of particulars.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The defendant's submissions were insufficient to establish, prima facie, that the plaintiff was informed of the risks, benefits, and alternatives to an endoscopic lumbar discectomy in a language that she could understand.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.chief_complaint: Pain and discomfort on the left side of the body
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient sought treatment for left-sided pain and discomfort.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Left-sided pain - differential includes lumbar disc pathology

requestables.name: Informed Consent Discussion
requestables.description: Discussion of risks, benefits, and alternatives to endoscopic lumbar discectomy

reveal.decision_impact: Impacts patient's understanding and consent validity

key_events.event: Patient seeks treatment for left-sided pain

decision_points.prompt: Which side should the discectomy be performed on?

options.description: Perform discectomy on the left side
options.clinical_reasoning: Patient's symptoms were on the left side

options.description: Perform discectomy on the right side

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant performed discectomy on the right side

expected_action_court.description: Discectomy should have been performed on the left side

explanation.what_should_have_happened: Procedure should have been on the left side

patient_outcome.description: Patient experienced nerve damage due to incorrect side surgery

key_findings: Breach of duty in performing surgery on the wrong side

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to perform surgery on correct side
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Surgery on left side would have addressed symptoms

factual_timeline.event: Patient seeks treatment for left-sided pain

allegations.allegation: Failure to perform surgery on correct side
